channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: HYDRAA Demolishes Illegal Structures Belonging to BRS Leader in Telangana
item.description: The Hyderabad Disaster Response and Asset Protection Agency (HYDRAA) carried out a significant demolition operation in Ameenpur, Telangana, on Saturday, April 11, 2026. The agency demolished a guest h...

item.title: Why Diplomacy Must Prevail Over Transactional Deal-Making in Global Politics
item.description: Michael Ignatieff argues that modern international relations are at risk when states treat all interactions as transactional deals rather than enduring commitments. Using the recent escalation of conf...

item.title: Mwanza Police Launches Operation Against Drunk and Unlicensed Drivers
item.description: In Mwanza, police have intensified efforts to combat road safety violations, particularly targeting drunk drivers and those without valid licenses. Alcohol impairs judgment, reaction time, and concent...

item.title: University of Ottawa lockdown ends after man arrested; no injuries reported
item.description: The University of Ottawa was placed on lockdown on Friday afternoon following reports of a violent threat on campus. Students were instructed to stop all activities and shelter inside buildings, with ...

item.title: Temporary CPUID Site Breach Swapped Legitimate Downloads with Malware
item.description: CPUID, the company behind popular tools like HWMonitor and CPU-Z, experienced a brief security incident where parts of its backend were hijacked by attackers. During the breach, which lasted approxima...

item.title: B.C. Premier Eby faces backlash from First Nations over proposal to temporarily suspend parts of DRIPA
item.description: First Nations leaders across British Columbia have denounced Premier David Eby’s proposal to temporarily suspend several sections of the province’s Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act ...

item.title: US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian delegation arrive in Islamabad for peace talks
item.description: United States Vice President JD Vance and his delegation arrived in Islamabad on Saturday morning for important peace talks with the Iranian leadership. The US team was warmly received at Nur Khan Air...

item.title: National University secures spot in UAAP baseball Finals after win over UP
item.description: National University (NU) booked its ticket to the UAAP Season 88 baseball Finals after defeating the University of the Philippines (UP) in a crucial semifinal showdown. The Bulldogs showcased solid pi...

item.title: Measles outbreak in Bangladesh linked to vaccination gaps and delayed immunisation drive
item.description: Bangladesh is facing a severe measles outbreak, with at least 143 people reported dead since March 15, most of them children, and over 12,000 suspected cases, marking the worst such crisis in the coun...

item.title: Lena Dunham reflects on rapid fame from Girls, personal struggles and her new memoir Famesick
item.description: In a candid interview with The Guardian, Lena Dunham discusses her new memoir Famesick, which details the intense pressures she faced after creating and starring in the hit HBO series Girls at the age...

item.title: Zanzibar and NIRAS partner to develop comprehensive water master plan
item.description: The government of Zanzibar has entered a landmark partnership with NIRAS International Consulting to prepare a comprehensive Water Master Plan that will improve the islands’ water supply, distribution...

item.title: Rivers Deputy Governor Calls for Transparency and Stronger Collaboration in Primary Healthcare
item.description: The Rivers State Deputy Governor, Prof. Ngozi Nma Odu, don call for better commitment, transparency, and strong collaboration between the Rivers State Contributory Health Protection Programme (RIVCHPP...

item.title: Islamabad High Court Considers Equitable Division of Marital Assets
item.description: Amara Waqas, a former PAF officer, married in 2015, faced difficulties in reclaiming property following her divorce in 2021. Despite contributing to the purchase of a Suzuki Cultus and bringing dowry ...

item.title: Eight hospitalized after chlorine gas leak at Victoria’s Crystal Pool
item.description: Eight individuals were hospitalized following a chlorine gas leak at Victoria’s aging Crystal Pool on Friday morning, prompting a brief shelter-in-place order for nearby residents. The incident occurr...

item.title: New Zealand declares emergencies and orders evacuations as Cyclone Vaianu approaches North Island
item.description: Thousands of residents in New Zealand's North Island have been ordered to evacuate their homes as the country prepares for the impact of Cyclone Vaianu. The storm is forecast to bring heavy rainfall a...

item.title: Trump’s Statements as Contempt Rather Than Lies: A Philosophical Perspective
item.description: Throughout his political career, Donald Trump has frequently made statements that are demonstrably false, from the birther conspiracy about Barack Obama to exaggerated claims about crowd sizes and pol...

item.title: Allan Alaalatoa returns as Brumbies aim to overcome recent inconsistencies
item.description: The ACT Brumbies edged out the Highlanders 14-10 in a hard-fought contest in Dunedin, with Allan Alaalatoa making a notable return after being sidelined for the entire season with a back injury. Alaal...

item.title: Supreme Court clarifies that voting and contesting elections are statutory rights under law
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has reiterated that the rights to vote and to contest elections are not fundamental rights, but statutory privileges granted through legislation. A bench comprising Justices...

item.title: CBN Announces New BVN Regulations Starting May 1 to Strengthen Banking Security
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) don release new rules wey go begin work from May 1, 2026, for all BVN holders across the country. The new BVN policy na part of CBN effort to reduce bank fraud, impro...

item.title: Authorities seize P64.4M of smuggled cigarettes in Sulu
item.description: A joint operation by the Philippine Navy and the Western Mindanao Command successfully intercepted a small boat carrying smuggled Indonesian cigarettes in the territorial waters of Sulu province on Ap...
